LAW AND MOTION, 301, DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION SUBMITTED ON OCT-30-2002 BY JAMES J. MCBRIDE. MOVING AND OPPOSING PARTIES APPEARED. CONTRA COSTA CLEAN WATER PROGRAM MADE A CCP 170.6 CHALLENGE TO JUDGE MCBRIDE. THE COURT TOOK THE MOTION UNDER SUBMISSION. PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO FILE AND DELIVER COPIES OF SIMULTANEOUS BRIEFS TO THE COURT AT 4:00 P.M. ON 11/4/2002. PERSONAL SERVICES ARE REQUIRED AMONG PARTIES. MOTION Notice; For Leave To File First Amended Verified Petition For Writs Of Mandate;
Off Calendar May-16-2003 Continued To Jun-10-03 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Michael Graf, Esq. of the LAW OFFICE OF THOMAS N. LIPPE 777-5600, for Petitioner San Francisco BayKeeper moving for leave to file first amended verified petition for writs of mandate
Carol A. Squire, DEPUTY ATTORNEY GENERAL 619/645-2219, for respondent and opposing party Regional Water Quality Control Board, et al.
Nicole E. Granquist, Eq. of DOWNEY, BRAND 916/444-1000, X-6369, for Real Party in Interest and opposing party Contra Costa Clean Water Program

RULING:
Upon request of counsel, the Court continued the petitioner's motion for leave to amend to June 10, 2003. Counsel stipulated to a new briefing schedule: Respondent and Real Parties to file the opposition to the writ on or before 7/3, petitioner's reply due on 7/15. Hearing on the writ is set on August 1, 2003. An order was signed in open court. (REQ/301/EH) Reporter: Beldon Yee CSR #2647
